title: argyrolobium zanonii subsp. stipulaceum
intro: Argyrolobium zanonii subsp. stipulaceum, also known as the South African or Cape yellow-flower, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the legume family. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, where it is valued for its medicinal properties, which include antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits associated with Argyrolobium zanonii subsp. stipulaceum include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, cough, and skin infections. The herbal preparations of this plant typically involve infusions or decoctions, which are made by steeping the leaves or roots in hot water.
